rationale.R1.2: The draft applies Peterson et al. (2010) NMC-derived degradation coefficients to an LFP fleet — an internal chemistry mismatch. Additionally, the concluding claim of 'positive net returns' is arithmetically falsified by the artifact's own Table 2 figures ($124 revenue − $187 degradation = −$63/year net loss); both constitute logical consistency failures central to any improvement.
rationale.R1.3: 'RegRank delivers positive net returns' is directly contradicted by the numbers in the immediately preceding paragraph ($124 − $187 = −$63). The evidence-claim gap is unambiguous and load-bearing: the paragraph cannot stand without resolving this contradiction.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' canonically encompasses prose quality; the artifact has correctable issues including passive constructions, redundant hedging ('now standard in current-generation'), and run-on sentence structure in the methodology paragraph.
rationale.R1.7: Peterson et al. (2010) is invoked as the source of per-cycle capacity-loss coefficients, but their paper characterises NMC chemistry, not LFP. Adopting those coefficients for an LFP simulation without adjustment or acknowledgment is a citation-accuracy and attribution problem that directly undermines the quantitative results.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement address substantive content errors — logical consistency, evidence-claim mismatches, and citation-accuracy issues — in addition to prose and style, or is this revision limited to writing-quality changes only?
